Name: AdamantineArmorHeavyEnchant
Description: Adamantine is extremely strong and favored by weapon and armor smiths alike for its ability to cut through solid barriers with ease and endure heavy blows. Armor made from adamantine grants its wearer damage reduction of 1/— if it's light armor, 2/— if it's medium armor, and 3/— if it's heavy armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons and armor made from it are always of masterwork quality; the masterwork cost is included in the prices given. Thus, adamantine weapons and ammunition have a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls, and the armor check penalty of adamantine armor is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary armor of its type. Items without metal parts cannot be made from adamantine. An arrow could be made of adamantine, but a quarterstaff could not.

Name: AdamantineWeaponEnchantment
Description: Mined from rocks that fell from the heavens, this ultrahard metal adds to the quality of a weapon or suit of armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons made from it are always of masterwork quality.

Name: AgainstHelplessPlus2
Description: Attacks with this weapon get an additional +2 enhancement bonus against stunned, paralyzed, nauseated and frightened targets.

Name: Agile
Description: Agile weapons are unusually well balanced and responsive. A wielder with the Weapon Finesse feat can choose to apply her Dexterity modifier to damage rolls with an agile weapon in place of her Strength modifier (whichever is higher). This modifier to damage is not increased for two-handed weapons, but is still reduced for off-hand weapons.

Name: Anarchic

Name: ArcaneArmorInvulnerabilityEnchant

Name: ArcaneArmorShadowEnchant

Name: ArcaneArmorShadowGreaterEnchant

Description: This creature takes 1d4 Constitution damage each turn. Bleeding can be stopped through the application of any effect that cures hit point damage (even if the bleed is ability damage).

Description: Whenever you score a critical hit with this weapon, your opponent becomes permanently blinded. A successful Fortitude save DC 15 reduces the duration for 1 round.

Name: BooksOfDreamsIIIStageEnchantment
Description: Luck always favors adventurers as well as this book. Whenever any of your summoned creatures die from an attack with a weapon, you and your allies restore 20 HP and 1 round of azata performance.

Name: BooksOfDreamsIIStageEnchantment
Description: As your travels continue and the story unravels itself, the book reveals more of its secrets. Now all your summoned creatures gain the effects of longstrider, greater and good hope spells.

Name: BooksOfDreamsIStageEnchantment
Description: While this book is active, each time you use an ability that lets you summon multiple creatures, their number is increased by one.

Name: Brass

Name: BrilliantEnergy
Description: A brilliant energy weapon ignores nonliving matter. Armor and shield bonuses to AC (including any enhancement bonuses to that armor) do not count against it because the weapon passes through armor. (Dexterity, deflection, dodge, natural armor, and other such bonuses still apply.) A brilliant energy weapon cannot harm undead, constructs, or objects.

Name: CallLightningCritical
Description: Any creature struck by a critical hit, either from a natural attack or from this scimitar, becomes a target of lightning, as per the Call Lightning Storm spell (5d6 damage, 24 DC Reflex half). All natural attacks from both the wielder of this scimitar and the wielder's animal companion have concussive and shocking modifiers applied to them. If the wielder is wild shaped into a bear or if their animal companion is a bear, the sonic and electrical damage from their attacks is increased to 1d10.

Name: CarroksThornRogueEnch
Description: This thorn can be wielded as a +2 destructive dagger. Whenever the wielder rolls a natural 20 on an attack roll made with this dagger, if the wielder is a rogue or a slayer of 7th level or higher or an assassin of any level, they inflict a negative level on the target.

Name: ChannelOnCrit
Description: Whenever you score a critical hit with this weapon, you emit a wave of negative energy that deals 4d6 damage to everyone around you. Creatures that take damage from negative energy receive a Will save DC 15 to halve the damage.

Whenever the wielder lands 3 consecutive hits on a fatigued enemy, that enemy must pass a Fortitude saving throw (DC 31) or become exhausted.

Name: FieryBody
Description: This spell transforms your body into living flame. You and your equipment are immune to fire damage. In fact, every time you would normally take damage from fire, you are instead healed of damage at a rate of 1 point per 3 points of damage the fire attack would have normally inflicted. You are immune toblindness, critical hits, ability score damage, disease, electricity, poison, and stunning. You have acid resistance 30. You take 150% as much damage from cold as normal. You have a +6 enhancement bonus to your Dexterity score. Your unarmed attack deals an additional 3d6 points of fire damage, and you are considered armed when making unarmed attacks. Fire spells you cast have their save DCs increased by +1.
Categories: AdditionalDamage, Critical, DC, Defensive, Elemental, PenaltiesAndCursed, Spellcasting, StatusAndCC

Name: FirebornWarhammerEnchantmentFire
Description: Greater Flaming weapon is sheathed in fire that deals an extra 2d6 points of fire damage on a successful hit and 1d10 on critical hit.

Name: Firebrand
Description: Flaming weapon is sheathed in fire that deals an extra 1d6 points of fire damage on a successful hit. The fire does not harm the wielder. The effect remains until another command is given.

Name: FireGemstone
Description: This staff contains six charges that are replenished each day. By spending three charges it wielder may cast fireball, and by spending two charges, they may cast scorching ray.

In addition to the extra 1d6 fire damage from the flaming ability, a flaming burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of fire damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of fire damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points of fire damage.

Name: ForcefulPushArrowsQuiverEnchantment
Description: The owner of this quiver can use it to shoot 30 units of enchanted ammunition. This ammunition deals additional 1d8 bludgeoning damage. Each time the projectile hits an enemy, the enemy must pass a Fortitude saving throw (DC 15 + wielder's Str modifier) or it will be pushed back on 5 feet and become prone.

Name: Fortification25Enchant
Description: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

Name: Fortification50Enchant
Description: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

Name: Fortification75Enchant
Description: This suit of armor or shield produces a magical force that protects vital areas of the wearer more effectively. When an enemy confirms a critical hit or lands a sneak attack against the wearer, there is a chance that the critical hit or sneak attack is negated and damage is instead rolled normally. The chance is 25% for light fortification, 50% for moderate fortification, and 75% for heavy fortification.

Name: Fortitude1
Description: Grants a +1 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Fortitude2
Description: Grants a +2 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Fortitude3
Description: Grants a +3 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Fortitude4
Description: Grants a +4 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Fortitude5
Description: Grants a +5 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Fortitude6
Description: Grants a +6 resistance bonus on Fortitude saving throws.

Name: Frost
Description: Frost weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 1d6 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

Name: Frost2d8
Description: Greater frost weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 2d8 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

Name: Furious
Description: A furious weapon serves as a focus for its wielder's anger. When the wielder is raging or under the effect of a rage spell, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than normal.

Name: Furyborn
Description: This special ability can only be placed on melee weapons. A furyborn weapon draws power from the anger and frustration the wielder feels when battling foes that refuse to die. Each time the wielder damages an opponent with the weapon, its enhancement bonus increases by +1 when making attacks against that opponent. Enhancement bonus can't be increased this way beyond the maximum value of +5. This extra enhancement bonus goes away if the opponent dies, the wielder uses the weapon to attack a different creature, or 1 hour passes.

Name: GentlePersuasionEnchant
Description: This armor is cursed to steer the wearer toward evil, choking the life out of them every time they or one of their associates does a good action, reducing their Constitution by 2 each time, to a minimum of 1. Only committing an evil act can provide relief, removing the Constitution penalty and giving 10 temporary hit points instead. Unequipping this armor is only possible after casting remove curse on the wearer and passing a DC 33 caster level check.

Name: GhostTouch
Description: A ghost touch weapon deals damage normally against incorporeal creatures, regardless of its bonus. An incorporeal creature's 50% reduction in damage from corporeal sources does not apply to attacks made against it with ghost touch weapons.

Name: GoodAligned
Description: A good-aligned weapon can bypass the DR of certain creatures.

Name: GreaterBaneEverything
Description: A bane weapon excels against certain foes. Against a designated foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus. It also deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against such foes.

Name: GreaterShadow
Description: This armor grants its wearer a +10 competence bonus to Stealth skill checks.

Name: HalfplateEnhancementRusty
Description: Broken armor gives half as much AC as it usually would. Its armor check penalty is doubled.

Name: Heartseeker
Description: A heartseeker weapon ignores the miss chance for concealment against most living targets, though the attack must still target the proper space. This special ability does not apply against aberrations, oozes, plants, outsiders with the elemental subtype, or any creature specifically noted to lack a heart.

Name: HeavyShieldEnhancementRusty
Description: Broken armor gives half as much AC as it usually would. Its armor check penalty is doubled.

Name: HideStandardEnhancementRusty
Description: Broken armor gives half as much AC as it usually would. Its armor check penalty is doubled.

Name: Holy
Description: A holy weapon is imbued with holy power. This power makes the weapon good-aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against all creatures of evil alignment.

Name: HumanbaneMorningstar2Enchant
Description: A bane weapon excels against certain foes. Against a designated foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus. It also deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against such foes.

Name: HundredPocktesEnchant
Description: This armor grants its wearer a +5 competence bonus to Trickery skill checks.

Name: Ice2d6
Description: Freezing weapon is sheathed in a terrible, icy cold that deals an extra 2d6 points of cold damage on a successful hit.

Name: IcyBurst
Description: An icy burst weapon functions as a frost weapon that also explodes with frost upon striking a successful critical hit. The frost does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra damage from the frost ability, an icy burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of cold damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of cold damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

Name: IgnitingEnchantment
Description: An igniting weapon functions as a flaming weapon that also causes the target to catch fire upon striking it with a successful critical hit. The target have to pass Fortitude saving throw (DC 20) or suffer 1d6 fire damage each round and can make a save each round on its turn to put out the fire.

Name: ImprovedCriticalMultiplerEnchantment
Description: Increases weapon's critical multiplier by one.

Description: Give +1 enhancement bonus to Intelligence

Name: Intelligence2

Name: Intelligence3

Name: JagannathKhandaMindAffectingEnchant
Description: The wielder of this weapon gets a +8 bonus on saving throws against mind-affecting effects.

Name: JagannathKhandaPoisonEnchant
Description: Creatures hit by the Khanda must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude saving throw or be affected by poison for 6 rounds. Poison deals 1d2 Wisdom damage and dazes a target for 1 round.

Name: JokeOfNatureArrowsQuiverEnchantment
Description: The owner of this quiver can use it to shoot 20 units of ammunition. This ammunition deals additional 1d6+5 piercing damage and on a confirmed critical hit transforms the enemy into a pony for 1 round.

Name: Keen
Description: Ability doubles the threat range of a weapon. This benefit doesn't stack with any other effects that expand the threat range of a weapon.

Name: LawfulAligned
Description: A lawful-aligned weapon can bypass the DR of certain creatures.

Name: LeatherStandardEnhancementRusty
Description: Broken armor gives half as much AC as it usually would. Its armor check penalty is doubled.

Name: LightShieldEnhancementRusty
Description: Broken armor gives half as much AC as it usually would. Its armor check penalty is doubled.

Name: LordProtectorEnchant
Description: A loyal weapon refuses to damage the allies of its wielder. Weapon's attack bonus is reduced by -5 against friendly targets.

Name: LuckSaves1
Description: Grants a +1 luck bonus on all saving throws.

Name: LuckyLongbowEnchant
Description: Critical multiplier of this weapon is ×4 instead of ×3.

Name: Mageblade
Description: When you have a touch spell charged, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus.

Name: MagicWeapon
Description: This enchantment makes the weapon magic and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction.

Name: Masterwork
Description: A masterwork weapon is a finely crafted version of a normal weapon. Wielding it provides a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls.

Name: MithralArmorEnchant
Description: Mithral is a very rare silvery, glistening metal that is lighter than steel but just as hard. When worked like steel, it becomes a wonderful material from which to create armor, and is occasionally used for other items as well. Most mithral armors are one category lighter than normal for purposes of movement and other limitations. Heavy armors are treated as medium, and medium armors are treated as light, but light armors are still treated as light. This decrease does not apply to proficiency in wearing the armor. Spell failure chances for armors and shields made from mithral are decreased by 10%, maximum Dexterity bonuses are increased by 2, and armor check penalties are decreased by 3 (to a minimum of 0). An item made from mithral weighs half as much as the same item made from other metals.

Name: MithralWeaponEnchantment
Description: Mithral is a rare, silvery metal that is lighter than steel but just as hard. An item made from mithral weighs half as much as the same item made from other metals. In the case of weapons, this lighter weight does not change a weapon's size category or the ease with which it can be wielded (whether it is light, one-handed, or two-handed). Mithral weapons count as silver for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. Weapons fashioned from mithral are always masterwork items as well.

Name: Mobility5
Description: Grants a +5 competence bonus on Mobility checks.

Name: NaturalArmorEnhancement1
Description: Grants a +1 enhancement bonus to existing natural armor.

Name: NaturalArmorEnhancement2
Description: Grants a +1 enhancement bonus to existing natural armor.

Description: Grants a +1 enhancement bonus to existing natural armor.

Description: Provides 15 points of resistance against negative energy damage.

Name: NegativeEnergyResistance10Enchant

Name: NegativeEnergyResistance20Enchant

Name: NegativeEnergyResistance30Enchant

Name: NullifyingEnchantment
Description: This special ability can only be placed on melee weapons. A nullifying weapon suppresses a creature's spell resistance for a short amount of time. Anytime the weapon strikes a creature with spell resistance, the target's spell resistance is reduced by 1 for 1 minute. On a confirmed critical hit, its spell resistance is instead reduced by 2. Multiple hits from a nullifying weapon stack.

Name: OolioddrooTongueEnchantment
Description: The victim takes 2 points of Wisdom drain. A creature can resist this attack with a successful DC 23 Will save. On a failed save, the oolioddroo targets the victim with one of the following effects. The save DC is Charisma-based.

Name: Oversized
Description: Weapon is exceptionally large and deals more damage, but attacks with it suffer a penalty of –2 to attack rolls.

Name: OversizedPenaltyNegateEnchant
Description: Weapon is exceptionally large and deals more damage, but attacks with it suffer a penalty of -2 to attack rolls.

Name: PatchworkHide
Description: This armor grants its wearer an inherent +3 damage bonus against animals, dragons and magical beasts.

Name: Perception5

Name: Persuasion4

Name: ProtectionFromEvil
Description: Weapon emits a Magic circle against evil effect. All creatures within the area gain the effects of a Protection from evil spell.

Name: QuickbladeEnchant
Description: When making a full attack, the wielder of a speed weapon may make two extra attack with it. The attacks use the wielder's full base attack bonus, plus any modifiers appropriate to the situation. (This benefit is not cumulative with similar effects, such as a haste spell.)

Name: Radiant
Description: Radiant weapon is imbued with life essence itself and deals an extra 1d6 points of positive energy damage on a successful hit.

Name: RawHolyPowerEnchantment
Description: This ring grants its wearer a +2 enhancement bonus to Strength ability score. The wearer gains a +2 enhancement bonus on attack rolls against undead. Once per day, the wearer of this ring can use it to cast the prayer spell with a duration of 5 rounds.

Name: Reflex1
Description: Grants a +1 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Reflex2
Description: Grants a +2 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Reflex3
Description: Grants a +3 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Reflex4
Description: Grants a +4 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Reflex5
Description: Grants a +5 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Reflex6
Description: Grants a +6 resistance bonus on Reflex saving throws.

Name: Resistance1
Description: Grants a +1 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance2
Description: Grants a +2 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance3
Description: Grants a +3 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance4
Description: Grants a +4 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance5
Description: Grants a +5 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance6
Description: Grants a +6 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: Resistance7
Description: Grants a +7 resistance bonus on all saving throws.

Name: RobeOfAirEnchant
Description: Grants +2 DC bonus to electrical spells.

Name: RobeOfEarthEnchant
Description: Grants +2 DC bonus to acid spells.

Name: RobeOfFireEnchant
Description: Whenever the wearer casts a spell with fire descriptor, its saving throw DC is increased by 2.

Name: RobeOfWaterEnchant
Description: Grants +2 DC bonus to cold spells.

Name: RodOfFiredVengenceEnchantment
Description: This rod is hot to touch. It vibrates slightly with the power burning inside. It transforms all wielder's spell damage into fire damage, increasing caster level by 1 and +3 bonus to Evocation school spells' DC. It also grants its wielder the ability to make up to five spells they cast per day maximized as though using the Maximize Spell feat. Greater rods can be used with spells of 9th level or lower.

Name: SacredVengenanceArrowsQuiverEnchantment
Description: The owner of this quiver can use it to shoot 30 units of ammunition. This ammunition hits the first enemy as well as two additional enemies, each of which must be within 15 feet of the primary target. It deals additional 1d6 points of holy damage.

Name: Sacrificial
Description: Against a helpless foe, the weapon's enhancement bonus is +2 better than its actual bonus.

Name: SarkorisDirgeEnchantment
Description: This +2 keen heavy mace of Sarkorian make deals an additional 2d8 holy damage to undead and grants its wielder a +2 bonus to Strength.

Name: SavesLuck2
Description: Grants a +2 luck bonus on all saving throws.

Name: SeriouslyEliteExpertise
Description: Wielder of this weapon performs ranged attacks as if they had Point-Blank Shot and Precise Shot feats.

Name: ShadowArmor
Description: This armor grants its wearer a +5 competence bonus to Stealth skill checks.

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus1
Description: Grants +1 enhancement bonus to Armor Class. The armor check penalty of magic shield is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary shield of its type.

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus2
Description: Grants +2 enhancement bonus to Armor Class. The armor check penalty of magic shield is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary shield of its type.

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus3
Description: Grants +3 enhancement bonus to Armor Class. The armor check penalty of magic shield is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary shield of its type.

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus4
Description: Grants +4 enhancement bonus to Armor Class. The armor check penalty of magic shield is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary shield of its type.

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus5

Name: ShieldEnhancementBonus6
Description: Grants +6 enhancement bonus to Armor Class. The armor check penalty of magic shield is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary shield of its type.

Name: Shock
Description: Shock weapon is sheathed in crackling electricity that deals an extra 1d6 points of electricity damage on a successful hit.

Name: Shock2d6
Description: Improved shock weapon is sheathed in crackling electricity that deals an extra 2d6 points of electricity damage on a successful hit.

Name: ShockingBurst
Description: A shocking burst weapon functions as a shock weapon that explodes with electricity upon striking a successful critical hit. The electricity does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra electricity damage from the shock ability, a shocking burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of electricity damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of electricity damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

Name: SilverWeaponEnchantment
Description: A complex process involving metallurgy and alchemy can bond silver to a weapon made of steel so that it bypasses the damage reduction of creatures such as lycanthropes. On a successful attack with a silver slashing or piercing weapon, the wielder takes a –1 penalty on the damage roll.

Name: SingingSteelEnchant
Description: This lustrous golden metal emits beautiful bell-like tones when struck. An alloy of gold and mithral, singing steel was originally created by the elves. Most armor made of singing steel is treated as one category lighter (heavy armors are treated as medium, and medium armors are treated as light, but light armors are still treated as light). The armor or shield's arcane spell failure chance is reduced by 5%, its maximum Dexterity bonus is increased by 1, and its armor check penalty is reduced by 1 (to a minimum of 0). The wielder or wearer can strike the singing steel as part of beginning a bardic performance. If the wielder can normally start a bardic performance as a standard action, he can do so as a move action instead. If the wielder can normally start a bardic performance as a move action, he can do so as a swift action instead. If the wielder can normally start a bardic performance as a swift action, he can do so as a free action instead.

Name: SlowOnHit14
Description: Whenever you score a hit with this weapon, your opponent becomes slowed for 1d4 rounds. A successful DC 14 Will save negates this. These effects don't stack. Additional hits instead add to the duration.

Name: Speed
Description: When making a full attack, the wielder of a speed weapon may make one extra attack with it. The attack uses the wielder's full base attack bonus, plus any modifiers appropriate to the situation. (This benefit is not cumulative with similar effects, such as a haste spell.)

Name: SpeedIncrease
Description: Grants a +10-foot enhancement bonus to base land speed.

Name: SpeedIncrease5
Description: Grants a +5-foot enhancement bonus to base land speed.

Name: SpellResistance13Enchant
Description: Grants spell resistance 13. Fixed price 10000 * (Value - 12)

Name: SpellResistance15Enchant
Description: Grants spell resistance 15. Fixed price 10000 * (Value - 12)

Description: Grants spell resistance 19. Fixed price 10000 * (Value - 12)

Name: SpellResistance32Enchant

Name: StrengthThrown
Description: This feature allows you to add your Strength bonus to damage with a bow.

Name: StunOnCrit17
Description: Whenever you score a critical hit with this weapon, your opponent becomes stunned for 1d4 rounds. A successful DC 17 Fortitude save reduces this to staggered for 1 round. These effects do not stack. Additional hits instead add to the duration.

Name: StunOnHit13
Description: Whenever you score a hit with this weapon, your opponent becomes stunned for 1 round. A successful DC 14 Fortitude save negates this. These effects do not stack. Additional hits instead add to the duration.

Name: ThroneKeeperEnchantment
Description: Adamantine is extremely strong and favored by weapon and armor smiths alike for its ability to cut through solid barriers with ease and endure heavy blows. Armor made from adamantine grants its wearer damage reduction of 1/— if it's light armor, 2/— if it's medium armor, and 3/— if it's heavy armor. Adamantine is so costly that weapons and armor made from it are always of masterwork quality; the masterwork cost is included in the prices given. Thus, adamantine weapons and ammunition have a +1 enhancement bonus on attack rolls, and the armor check penalty of adamantine armor is lessened by 1 compared to ordinary armor of its type. Items without metal parts cannot be made from adamantine. An arrow could be made of adamantine, but a quarterstaff could not.

Name: Thundering
Description: A thundering weapon creates a cacophonous roar like thunder whenever it strikes a target, dealing an extra 1d6 points of sonic damage.

Name: ThunderingBurst
Description: A thundering burst weapon functions as a thundering weapon that explodes with sonic upon striking a successful critical hit. The sonic does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra sonic damage from the thundering ability, a thundering burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of sonic damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of sonic damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points.

Name: TouchEnchant2
Description: Owner of this item gets +2 enhancement bonus to attack with touch spells.

Name: TowerShieldAttackPenalty
Description: When employing a tower shield in combat, you take a –2 penalty on attack rolls because of the shield's encumbrance.

Name: Ultrasound
Description: An ultrasound weapon creates a hardly hearable noise whenever it strikes a target, dealing an extra 2d6 points of sonic damage.

Name: UnarmedAgileEnchantment
Description: Prerequisite: Weapon Finesse. Apply Dexterity modifier instead of Strength to unarmed strike damage.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement1
Description: Grants a +1 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement2
Description: Grants a +2 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement3
Description: Grants a +3 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement4
Description: Grants a +4 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement5
Description: Grants a +5 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: UnarmedEnhancement6
Description: Grants a +6 enhancement bonus on unarmed attack and damage rolls.

Name: Unholy
Description: An unholy weapon is imbued with unholy power. This power makes the weapon evil-aligned and thus bypasses the corresponding damage reduction. It deals an extra 2d6 points of damage against all creatures of good alignment.

Name: UnstoppableEnchantment
Description: This weapon ignores all damage reduction and immunities.

Name: ViciousEnchantment
Description: When a vicious weapon strikes an opponent, it creates a flash of disruptive energy that resonates between the opponent and the wielder. This energy deals an extra 2d6 points of damage to the opponent and 1d6 points of damage to the wielder.

Name: Vorpal
Description: This potent and feared ability allows the weapon to sever the heads of those it strikes. Upon a roll of natural 20 (followed by a successful roll to confirm the critical hit), the weapon severs the opponent's head (if it has one) from its body. Some creatures, such as many aberrations and all oozes, have no heads. Others, such as golems and undead creatures other than vampires, are not affected by the loss of their heads. Most other creatures, however, die when their heads are cut off.

Name: WarswordEnchantmentTarget
Description: When a vicious weapon strikes an opponent, it creates a flash of disruptive energy that resonates between the opponent and the wielder. This energy deals an extra 2d6 points of damage to the opponent and 1d6 points of damage to the wielder.

Name: WeaponBondFlamingBurstEnchant
Description: A flaming burst weapon functions as a flaming weapon that also explodes with flame upon striking a successful critical hit. The fire does not harm the wielder. In addition to the extra 1d6 fire damage from the flaming ability, a flaming burst weapon deals an extra 1d10 points of fire damage on a successful critical hit. If the weapon's critical multiplier is ×3, add an extra 2d10 points of fire damage instead, and if the multiplier is ×4, add an extra 3d10 points of fire damage.
